description: Curated carrier status gene panel for GenomeInsight (P3-35). Covers 5 autosomal recessive genes plus 2 dual-role AD genes (BRCA1/BRCA2) for reproductive carrier screening.

genes.name: Cystic Fibrosis Transmembrane Conductance Regulator
genes.notes: Most common autosomal recessive condition in populations of European descent. F508del (rs113993960) accounts for ~70% of pathogenic alleles. Over 2000 known CFTR variants.

genes.name: Hemoglobin Subunit Beta
genes.notes: HbS (rs334, Glu6Val) causes sickle cell disease. Beta-thalassemia caused by >200 known mutations. Carrier frequency highest in African, Mediterranean, and Southeast Asian populations.

genes.name: Glucosylceramidase Beta
genes.notes: Most common lysosomal storage disorder. N370S (rs76763715) and L444P (rs421016) are the most prevalent pathogenic variants. Carrier frequency ~1:15 in Ashkenazi Jewish population.

genes.name: Hexosaminidase Subunit Alpha
genes.notes: Classic Tay-Sachs disease is a severe infantile-onset neurodegenerative condition. Carrier frequency ~1:30 in Ashkenazi Jewish, French-Canadian, and Cajun populations.

genes.name: BRCA1 DNA Repair Associated
conditions: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
genes.notes: Dual-role gene: produces both cancer predisposition finding (disease risk, Cancer module) and carrier status finding (reproductive risk, Carrier module). Included in carrier panel for reproductive planning context — heterozygous carriers may pass variant to offspring.

genes.name: BRCA2 DNA Repair Associated
conditions: Hereditary Breast and Ovarian Cancer Syndrome
genes.notes: Dual-role gene: produces both cancer predisposition finding (disease risk, Cancer module) and carrier status finding (reproductive risk, Carrier module). Included in carrier panel for reproductive planning context — heterozygous carriers may pass variant to offspring.

genes.name: Survival Motor Neuron 1
genes.notes: Most common genetic cause of infant mortality. ~95% of cases caused by homozygous SMN1 exon 7 deletion (not detectable by SNP array — coverage may be Insufficient). Carrier frequency ~1:50 across populations. Point mutations account for ~5% of pathogenic alleles.
